text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

Selected Team GB members are given the added honour of carrying their delegation's flag during the opening ceremony

TheParis 2024 Olympicsare set to commence, with over a fortnight’s action showcasing some of the globe’s best sporting talent.

Theopening ceremonywill begin this evening with more than 10,000 athletes, representing 206 countries, sailing along theRiver Seineas part of a flotilla of more than 90 boats.

It marks the first time in the history of the Olympic Summer Games that the ceremony will be held outside a stadium.

Around 600,000 spectators will watch as the procession makes its way down the river on a tour of Parisian landmarks.

And over 100 heads of state, royals and other dignitaries will also be in attendance.

French PresidentEmmanuel Macronwill officially open the Games, alongside the lighting of the Olympic cauldron with the Olympic flame.

British viewers wanting to tune in to the action can watch on BBC and Discovery+ from 6.30pm tonight BST.

The Games will officially be launched at 7.24pm BST.

For the Paris 2024 Olympics, the Team GB flag bearers are diverTom Daleyand rowerHelen Glover.

Daley captured the hearts of the nation when he made his debut for Team GB at the Beijing 2008 Olympic Games at the tender age of 14.

The four-time Olympic medallist will contest his fifth Olympics after ending his long wait for gold at Tokyo 2020 in the men’s 10-metre synchro.

The 30-year-old said his first thoughts were of his father, Robert, who died of cancer one year before his son won the first of his four Olympic medals in 2012.

“My dad would not have ever believed I would be a flag-bearer one day,” Daley said.

“He always con­sidered flag-bearers to be Olympic icons and legends. I think he would be so incredibly proud of me being a flag-bearer of Team GB.

“I am extremely proud,” he said.

Glover, who is competing at her fourth Games, described carrying the British flag as “the biggest honour of my life”.

The three-time world champion, who will compete in Paris as part of the women’s four, became the first mother to row for Great Britain at an Olympics when she finished fourth alongside Polly Swann in the women’s pairs in Tokyo.

“I never thought I would be an Olympian,” she said.

“Being part of history as a flag-bearer… I haven’t really got my head round it yet.

“Everyone I have told so far has cried, and I cried when I was told, which goes to show how much of an honour it is and how much it means.”

The rower retired from the sport after her second gold medal at the Rio 2016 Olympics. She gave birth to her first child Logan in 2018, and twins Kit and Bo in January 2020, before returning to compete in the Tokyo Olympics.

The women’s four won the European Championships in April and World Cup gold in Varese and Lucerne this year. They are odds-on favourites to win gold, which would make Glover only the third British woman in history to win three Olympic gold medals, and only the second to do so across three different Games.

Taking part in the Olympic Games ranks as one of the greatest moments in any athlete’s career. A chosen few enjoy the added honour of being selected to carry the flag of their delegation during the opening ceremony.

Sir Andrew Murray, Sir Chris Hoy, Kate Howey and Sir Matthew Pinsent are just some of the British athletes to have carried out the role.

Team GB flag bearers are chosen by the British Olympic Association. The decision is also sometimes opened up to other British sporting agencies.

Each sport involved in Team GB are permitted to nominate one of their own competitors then to vote on the short-list.

This means that the process is open to a wider group of people and can involve the competitors themselves.

Team GB describes its flag-bearers as the “highest honour” it can bestow, with the list of former athletes a who’s who of trail-blazers and history-makers.

“Tom and Helen exemplify and embody the values of Team GB and our great country,” said Team GB Chef de Mission Mark England.

“They are dedicated, hard-working, talented athletes, who have achieved greatness in their sports and have captured the affection of the nation in doing so.

“They are both truly proud to wear the Union flag and I’ve had the pleasure of seeing that first hand at every Olympic Games they have attended. It is an absolute privilege to name them as flag-bearers for Team GB at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.”

There are 327 athletes competing in the Paris 2024 Olympics under the Team GB banner.

And for only the second time in Team GB’s history, there will be more women competing than men at Paris, with 155 male and 172 female athletes.

Of the competitors, 169 are returning Olympians, while 74 are returning Olympic medallists. Of this second group, 33 can call themselves Olympic champions.

Adam Peaty(swimming) andMax Whitlock(gymnastics) are the most decorated members of team GB when it comes to Olympic titles, with both of them boasting a hat-trick of Olympic crowns to their names.

Until recently, equestrian Charlotte Dujardin – Team GB’s joint most decorated female Olympian – joined them in this top tier, but wasprovisionally suspended over animal cruelty allegations.

Video footage from four years ago shows Dujardin repeatedly whipping a horse.

“What happened was completely out of character and does not reflect how I train my horses or coach my pupils, however there is no excuse,” she said in a statement. “I am deeply ashamed and should have set a better example in that moment.”

Elsewhere, Duncan Scott is the most decorated at a single Games after winning four medals at Tokyo 2020.

Sky Brownmade history after becoming Team GB’s youngest-ever Olympic medallist by winning skateboarding park bronze at Tokyo 2020 at just 13 years old. She returns to Paris 2024 and remains the youngest member of the team.

Brown is one of 14 teenage athletes competing for Team GB in this year’s event.

The oldest competitor in Team GB is 57-year-old Carl Hester who will also make history by equalling fellow equestrian legend Nick Skelton’s record of seven Olympic Games when he competes at Paris.

Impartial news + intelligent debate

title: Who are the Paris 2024 Olympic Team GB flag-bearers?
author: Joanna Whitehead
description: Selected Team GB members are given the added honour of carrying their delegation's flag during the opening ceremony
